title: Buttery Shattering Spanish Chicken Tapas Platter
description: Indulgent Spanish dish featuring peppercorns and crispy chicken served as a tapas platter. Perfect for a dinner gathering with friends.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt, black peppercorns, and smoked paprika.
Heat the butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the chicken thighs until browned on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the minced garlic to the skillet, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the thinly sliced onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken back to the skillet and squeeze the lemon juice over the top.
Continue cooking for an additional 5-6 minutes or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Slice the chicken into thin strips and serve immediately.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve as a tapas platter.
